content.Text:
[Tue 2026-04-21 03:45 PDT] Create a new "Protocol Evolution Engine" page for this BioBots bioprinting dashboard app.

FILE TO CREATE: docs/protocol-evolution.html

This is a SINGLE self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JS (no external dependencies except docs/shared/commandPalette.js and docs/shared/utils.js which are optional). Follow the exact same dark theme pattern used in the other HTML files in docs/ folder.

THEME/STYLE: Use the same CSS variables and styling as other pages:
- --bg:#0a0e17, --surface:#131a2b, --surface2:#1a2340, --border:#2a3555, --text:#e0e6f0
- --accent:#00d4aa, --accent2:#00b4d8, --warn:#f0a030, --danger:#f04040, --success:#00d46a
- Cards with border-radius:12px, .tag classes, same button styles etc.

FEATURE DESCRIPTION:
The Protocol Evolution Engine autonomously tracks protocol versions over time and provides intelligent insights.

CORE FUNCTIONALITY:

1. **Protocol Version Timeline** - Users create protocol entries with:
   - Protocol name, version number, date
   - Key parameters (temperature, pressure, speed, duration, material concentration)
   - Performance metrics (yield %, viability %, print quality 1-10, reproducibility 1-10)
   - Notes field
   - Store in localStorage keyed by protocol name

2. **Performance Trend Detection** (autonomous):
   - When a protocol has 3+ versions, auto-analyze trends using linear regression on each metric
   - Detect degradation (declining yield/viability/quality) with slope analysis
   - Detect improvement trends
   - Show trend arrows (↑ improving, ↓ degrading, → stable) with color coding

3. **Degradation Alerts** (proactive):
   - Auto-scan all protocols and flag concerning patterns:
     - Yield dropped >5% over last 3 versions
     - Viability declining
     - Quality score dropping
     - Parameter drift (settings changing without improvement)
   - Show alert cards with severity (warning/critical)
   - Suggest which parameter changes correlate with degradation

4. **Improvement Recommender** (agentic):
   - Based on best-performing version, recommend parameter targets for next version
   - Identify which parameter changes led to best results (correlation analysis)
   - Generate a "Next Version Blueprint" with specific parameter suggestions
   - Confidence scoring for each recommendation

5. **Interactive Canvas Chart**:
   - Multi-line chart showing metrics across versions for selected protocol
   - Overlay parameter changes as markers
   - Forecast line (dashed) projecting next version's expected metrics

6. **Dashboard View**:
   - Fleet overview of all protocols with health badges (Thriving/Stable/Declining/Critical)
   - Sort by health status
   - Quick stats: total protocols, total versions, avg health score

7. **Demo Data**: Include a "Load Demo" button that populates 3 sample protocols with 5+ versions each showing different patterns (one improving, one stable, one degrading).

8. **Export**: JSON export of all protocol evolution data.

UI LAYOUT:
- Top: Title "Protocol Evolution Engine" with subtitle "Autonomous protocol performance tracking & improvement recommendations"
- Header stats row (total protocols, versions, avg health, alerts count)
- Tab bar: Dashboard | Timeline | Analysis | Recommendations
- Dashboard tab: protocol fleet cards with health badges
- Timeline tab: select protocol, see version timeline with Canvas chart
- Analysis tab: degradation alerts and trend analysis
- Recommendations tab: next-version blueprints

Also update docs/index.html: add a link card for protocol-evolution.html in the appropriate section. Look at how other tools are linked and follow the same pattern. The card should say "Protocol Evolution" with description "Autonomous protocol version tracking with trend detection & improvement recommendations" and icon 🧬.

Also update docs/sitemap.xml to include protocol-evolution.html.

IMPORTANT:
- The page must be completely self-contained (inline CSS + JS)
- Include the commandPalette.js script tag at bottom like other pages
- Use Canvas API for the chart (no chart libraries)
- Make it work standalone - no build step needed
- Total file should be under 1500 lines
- Run `node -c index.js` to verify the main JS file still parses after any changes
